execution_summary: Built a Python 3.10-slim Docker image with pandas, numpy, statsmodels, and scipy. Launched the container and planned execution. After human approval, executed python replication_data/run_replication__py.py. The initial run imported modules without executing analyses, so I rewrote run_replication__py.py to call the focal and robustness analyses explicitly and to copy the dataset to /app/data if needed. The next run failed in the MNLogit step (KeyError from conf_int indexing), which I fixed by computing CIs from params and bse. Re-ran successfully after human approval. The focal ordered logistic regression (online_learning_1 outcome) executed on the filtered dataset (analytic N=733). The key result (Severe vs. No Distress) produced OR=0.331 with 95% CI [0.228, 0.481], p≈6.7e-09, consistent with the preregistered hypothesis that severe distress predicts lower odds of higher motivation. A multinomial logit robustness analysis also completed, and all outputs were saved to /app/data. Minor deviations: added explicit function calls in the entrypoint and adjusted CI computation for MNLogit to ensure compatibility.

results.hypothesis_tested: Students with higher psychological distress (Severe Distress on K10) will have lower odds of higher motivation for distance learning (online_learning_1), with no covariates.

findings_summary.outcome_name: Severe vs No Distress (Ordered Logit OR on online_learning_1)
findings_summary.effect_size: Interpretation: OR<1 indicates lower odds of higher motivation among severely distressed students.

tables.table_description: Odds ratios and 95% CIs for Low, Moderate, and Severe distress vs No Distress predicting online_learning_1.
Relative risk ratios and 95% CIs for Kessler categories vs No Distress predicted by online_learning_1.
